description: You are helping a field-ecology research group tidy up the way they track raw datasets and the parameters used to process them. When you finish, three things must exist: 1. A YAML inventory file at /home/user/project/config/datasets.yaml 2. A TOML processing-parameter file at /home/user/project/config/processing.toml 3. A plain-text log file that documents your actions at /home/user/project/logs/config_update.log • The project root (/home/user/project) and its two sub-directories, config and logs, do not exist yet. • All files mentioned in this task must be created from scratch by the agent. • No root privileges are available, so stay inside /home/user. 1. Final YAML file – /home/user/project/config/datasets.yaml The file must be valid YAML and follow this exact structure and key order: ```yaml datasets: - name: coral_reef path: /data/ocean/coral_reef format: images size_gb: 12 - name: forest_canopy path: /data/forest/canopy format: lidar size_gb: 25 - name: urban_landscapes path: /data/urban/landscapes format: videos size_gb: 40 ``` Notes: • Indentation must be two spaces. • Key spelling and case must match exactly. 2. Final TOML file – /home/user/project/config/processing.toml The file must be valid TOML. Keep the order shown below and use double quotes for all strings: ```toml [default] threads = 4 output_dir = "/home/user/project/output" [[dataset]] name = "coral_reef" preprocess = "normalize" resolution = "4k" [[dataset]] name = "forest_canopy" preprocess = "denoise" resolution = "high" [[dataset]] name = "urban_landscapes" preprocess = "stabilize" resolution = "1080p" ``` Notes: • There must be an empty line between each top-level element or array-of-table block. • Exactly three [[dataset]] blocks must appear in the order shown. 3. Log file – /home/user/project/logs/config_update.log Each line documents one milestone in the format ``` 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | ``` where • the timestamp is the literal string 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z (do NOT use the current time) • is one of the four action keywords below • is exactly the text indicated in the examples Required four lines, in this order: 1. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | CREATED /home/user/project/config/datasets.yaml 2. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | ADDED urban_landscapes to datasets.yaml 3. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | CREATED /home/user/project/config/processing.toml 4. 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | APPENDED urban_landscapes section to processing.toml Assertions the automated grader will make: • The file exists and contains exactly four newline-terminated lines. • Each line starts with the static timestamp above. • The four ACTION keywords appear in the required order. 1. Create the directory hierarchy • /home/user/project • /home/user/project/config • /home/user/project/logs 2. Build the initial YAML file that lists two datasets (coral_reef and forest_canopy). 3. Update the YAML file by appending the third dataset, urban_landscapes, so the final state matches the specification above. 4. Build the initial TOML file that contains the [default] section and two [[dataset]] tables for the first two datasets. 5. Extend the TOML file by appending the [[dataset]] table for urban_landscapes. 6. Produce the log file and write the four mandatory lines exactly as specified. 7. Double-check all three files for typing, spacing, and ordering, because the automated grader will compare the files byte-for-byte. • If the grader cannot locate any of the three files at the paths provided, the task will fail immediately. • The grader uses a strict comparison; even stray whitespace will cause a mismatch. • Use simple tools such as printf, echo, sed, or temporary files plus mv to ensure exact content. • Remember to terminate every line (including the last) with a newline character.
truth:
<!--
    The information below is hidden from the agent. Automated tests rely on it.
  -->
  <final-state>
    <directories>
      /home/user/project
      /home/user/project/config
      /home/user/project/logs
    </directories>

    <files>

      <file path="/home/user/project/config/datasets.yaml"><![CDATA[
datasets:
  - name: coral_reef
    path: /data/ocean/coral_reef
    format: images
    size_gb: 12
  - name: forest_canopy
    path: /data/forest/canopy
    format: lidar
    size_gb: 25
  - name: urban_landscapes
    path: /data/urban/landscapes
    format: videos
    size_gb: 40
]]></file>

      <file path="/home/user/project/config/processing.toml"><![CDATA[
[default]
threads = 4
output_dir = "/home/user/project/output"

[[dataset]]
name       = "coral_reef"
preprocess = "normalize"
resolution = "4k"

[[dataset]]
name       = "forest_canopy"
preprocess = "denoise"
resolution = "high"

[[dataset]]
name       = "urban_landscapes"
preprocess = "stabilize"
resolution = "1080p"
]]></file>

      <file path="/home/user/project/logs/config_update.log"><![CDATA[
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | CREATED /home/user/project/config/datasets.yaml
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | ADDED urban_landscapes to datasets.yaml
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | CREATED /home/user/project/config/processing.toml
2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | APPENDED urban_landscapes section to processing.toml
]]></file>

    </files>
  </final-state>
